title: Reusable preference summary partial for admin/captain-side consumption
description: Build a single template partial that renders a compact, informative summary of a player's 'My Tennis' answers. Consumed by the admin player detail page (WI-099) and the Sprint 017 captain dashboard — never by any /my-profile/* or /my-availability/* handler. This is the canonical read-side render of stored preferences.
acceptance_criteria: New partial at templates/admin/partials/my_tennis_summary.html renders a player's stored preferences compactly
Partial renders gracefully when most/all fields are empty ('Not filled in yet — that's ok.')
Partial uses chips / pill tags for enum values (handedness, court side, match-type appetite, etc.)
Partial shows partner lists (clicks_with, would_love_to_try) with full names where resolvable
Partial does NOT render captain_player_notes — that's a separate surface (Sprint 017 WI-105)
Partial has a `scope` parameter controlling whether sensitive fields (what_to_know_about_my_game, notes_to_captain) are shown — default yes for admin/captain consumers
Service method GetPlayerTennisSummary(playerID) returns the composite struct needed by the partial
Partial file path is documented so Sprint 017 can include it directly from the dashboard

files_to_create: internal/admin/service_player_tennis.go — wraps repository calls for preferences + partner lists
technical_notes.positive_framing_in_copy: The partial celebrates rather than audits — 'Loves the net', 'Thrives under pressure' not 'Baseline only / avoids pressure'

testing_requirements: Unit test: partial renders with fully-populated preferences
Unit test: partial renders with zero preferences (friendly empty state)
Unit test: partial renders with partial preferences (subset of fields)
Visual QA: layout is compact enough to fit multiple summaries side-by-side on the Sprint 017 dashboard

context.user_story: As a captain, I want a consistent, glanceable summary of each player's tennis across every surface where I see them — the player detail page and the planning dashboard.
